policy: Only include public-domain, U.S. federal government, CC0, or explicitly approved permissive/attribution sources in the active corpus. Keep SCP/CC-BY-SA material out of the active corpus unless the project deliberately accepts share-alike obligations.

sources.title: World English Bible
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Public-domain English Bible translation via Project Gutenberg. Use as symbolic and scriptural motif source; avoid long direct quotation in generated prose.

sources.title: JPS TaNaKH 1917
sources.license: Public Domain
sources.notes: 1917 Jewish Publication Society translation. Public-domain Tanakh/Torah anchor; eBible details page also offers HTML, EPUB, plain-text chapter files, VPL, USFX, and USFM packages.

sources.title: The Koran, translated by J. M. Rodwell
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Project Gutenberg public-domain USA Quran translation. Translation style is older; use primarily for motifs and structure.

sources.title: National Incident Management System, Third Edition
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Procedural realism source for command, coordination, incident management, resource flow, and SITREP structure. Check document for third-party marks before quoting.

sources.title: Emergency Operations Center Quick Reference Guide
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Procedural source for operations center language, reporting roles, and interagency coordination.

sources.title: OSHA Hazardous Waste Operations and Emergency Response guidance
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Containment and field-safety procedural language. Use for PPE, zones, decontamination, and response posture.

sources.title: SCP Wiki material
sources.license: CC BY-SA 3.0
sources.notes: Excluded from active corpus for now. Add only if the project accepts attribution and share-alike obligations for derivative content. Avoid SCP-173 former image.

sources.title: MCRP 3-10A.4 Marine Rifle Squad
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S.; publicly releasable Marine Corps doctrine
sources.notes: Small-unit tactical source for squad roles, formations, actions on contact, fire/maneuver, and field vocabulary. Use for procedural texture, not tactical instruction to players. Inactive for now: official source verified, but direct terminal download returned HTTP 403 in Termux; do not use mirror copies without review.

sources.title: MCRP 3-10A.3 Marine Infantry Platoon
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S.; publicly releasable Marine Corps doctrine
sources.notes: Platoon-level planning and operating considerations for patrols, security, movement, coordination, and leader decision language. Inactive for now: official source verified, but direct terminal download returned HTTP 403 in Termux; do not use mirror copies without review.

sources.title: MCRP 3-30.7 Commander’s Tactical Handbook
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S.; distribution statement A, approved for public release
sources.notes: Useful for five-paragraph orders, troop-leading steps, patrol planning, cordon/search, roadblocks, communications, and concise command language. Inactive for now: official source verified, but direct terminal download returned HTTP 403 in Termux; do not use mirror copies without review.

sources.title: Guideline for Isolation Precautions: Preventing Transmission of Infectious Agents in Healthcare Settings
sources.license: Public Domain / U.S. federal government CDC work
sources.notes: Isolation, transmission-based precautions, PPE, infection-control escalation, and quarantine-adjacent procedures.

sources.title: Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities
sources.license: Public Domain / U.S. federal government CDC work
sources.notes: Air, water, surface, environmental sampling, remediation, and facility infection-control language.

sources.title: Interim Guidelines for Hospital Response to Mass Casualties from a Radiological Incident
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Hospital surge, self-triage, contamination screening, assessment centers, decontamination, and crisis communications under mass-casualty pressure.

sources.title: A Guide to Managing Stress for Disaster Responders and First Responders
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Responder fatigue, organizational stress management, symptoms, peer support, supervisor intervention, and return-to-work texture. Inactive for now: official source verified, but direct terminal download returns HTTP 403 in this environment.

sources.title: Tips for Disaster Responders: Preventing and Managing Stress
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Short field-oriented stress signs and management strategies for emergency responders. Inactive for now: official source verified, but direct terminal download returns HTTP 403 in this environment.

sources.title: The Golden Bough: A Study of Magic and Religion
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Comparative motifs: sacred kingship, ritual substitution, contagious/sympathetic magic, taboos, seasonal death/rebirth. Treat Frazer as historical source, not modern anthropology.

sources.title: The Legends of the Jews, Volume 1
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Midrashic/legendary expansion around Genesis figures and events; useful for symbolic variants without relying only on canonical scripture.

sources.title: The Book of Enoch the Prophet, Richard Laurence translation
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Apocalyptic and angelological motifs: Watchers, heavenly gates, judgment, measurement, celestial order, forbidden instruction.

sources.title: The Lesser Key of Solomon, Goetia
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Occult structure source for seals, names, offices, protective implements, procedural ritual language, and dangerous catalog logic. Do not copy ritual sequences wholesale.

sources.title: The Colour Out of Space
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Environmental anomaly source for contaminated wells, agricultural blight, family deterioration, impossible color, delayed evidence, and official uncertainty. Use for SCP-adjacent investigation texture, not named mythos.

sources.title: The Dunwich Horror
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Rural archive, hidden lineage, oversized absence, livestock signs, restricted books, and partial expert interpretation. Keep the output restrained and avoid reproducing period prejudice or dialect caricature.

sources.title: The Shadow over Innsmouth
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Community-scale contamination, institutional delay, local secrecy, maritime pressure, genealogy, and raid aftermath. Use as structure for civil containment and inherited anomaly arcs, not as creature lore.

sources.title: At the Mountains of Madness
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Expedition reports, impossible architecture, scientific caution, field notes, fossil/biological ambiguity, and awe under professional language. Use for Institute survey cadence and scale mismatch.

sources.title: The Shunned House
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Single-site anomaly investigation, recurring sickness, cellar focus, municipal history, instrumentation, and procedural descent. Useful for contained mission rooms and follow-up escalation.

sources.title: The Case of Charles Dexter Ward
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA
sources.notes: Archive investigation, identity drift, names as operational hazards, old correspondence, hidden laboratories, and case-file structure. Good fit for Revelation records, naming, and resurrection motifs.

sources.title: SH 21-76 Ranger Handbook
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA; Wikimedia Commons file identifies this as a U.S. Army government work and links the official Army source
sources.notes: Tactical source for patrol structure, leader duties, movement, security, communications, and concise field language. Downloaded via Wikimedia Commons because official Army endpoints failed from Termux; Commons metadata identifies the source as U.S. Government public domain.

sources.title: ATP 3-06 Urban Operations
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S. unless third-party material is marked
sources.notes: Urban operation doctrine for dense terrain, buildings, civilians, infrastructure, movement constraints, and command/control friction. Inactive for now: official Army Publishing download returned an HTML error page in this environment.

sources.title: CALL 21-14 Building Cohesive Teams
sources.license: U.S. federal government work / public domain in the U.S.; approved for public release
sources.notes: Leadership, conflict, team climate, stressors, compliance vs commitment. Useful for squad morale, refusal, concessions, and mutiny-adjacent mechanics.

sources.title: ATP 3-21.8 Infantry Rifle Platoon and Squad
sources.license: Public Domain in the USA; Wikimedia Commons file identifies this as a U.S. Army government work and links the official Army source
sources.notes: Small-unit doctrine for squad/platoon roles, planning, responsibilities, control, movement, sustainment, and tactical reporting vocabulary. Use for fictional procedural texture, not player-facing tactical instruction.

sources.title: Traumatic Incident Stress: Information for Emergency Response Workers
sources.license: Public Domain / U.S. federal government CDC-NIOSH work
sources.notes: Short responder stress source covering symptoms, on-site response, home recovery, and when stress becomes operationally relevant.

sources.title: Judgment and Decision Making Under Stress: An Overview for Emergency Managers
sources.license: Public Domain / U.S. federal government CDC-NIOSH work
sources.notes: Emergency decision-making under stress; useful for hidden-stat officer/squad outcomes, hesitation, overload, tunnel vision, and degraded command judgment.
